sections.paragraph: an unused root; nearly the same as Arab. كسر (the root كزر, which is found in Simonis, is altogether wanting in Arabic), to break with violence, to rout an enemy, med. Kesra, to be angry, followed by على; compare Syriac ܟܙܝܺܪܳܐ, bold, daring. (Kindred roots are קָצַר; גָּזַר, גָּזַל.) Hence אַכְזָר, אַכְזָרִי, אַכְזְרִיּוּת.
